arts.ttl: Brentford v Tottenham: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Game at Gtech Community Stadium starts at 2pm (GMT) 10 things to look out for this weekend in the top flight Share your thoughts with Daniel via email Brentford make two changes to the side that won at Palace last weekend. Out go Flekken and, at right-back, Mads Roerslev has beetled off to Wolfsburg on loan, so Kristoffer Ajer comes in. Ah but first of all, Thomas Frank explains that Mark Flekken has a side strain so won’t play today but he’s every confidence in his replacement, Hákon Valdimarsson, did well coming on last week. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou explains Micky van de Ven absence from Tottenham squad as Liverpool fear raised
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou explains why Micky van de Ven is missing from Tottenham Hotspur's squad for Sunday's Premier League London derby against Brentford.

arts.ttl: Why Micky van de Ven is not playing for Tottenham against Brentford
arts.sum: Micky van de Ven returned for Tottenham against Elfsborg in midweek but was a notable absentee from the team to face Brentford.

arts.ttl: Tottenham: Ange Postecoglou explains Micky van de Ven absence against Brentford
arts.sum: Van de Ven missing for Spurs at the Gtech Community Stadium after injury comeback

arts.ttl: Spurs confirmed team vs Brentford - Van de Ven out but Spence and Moore start
arts.sum: Spurs only recently got the Dutch defender back into the team but he is not involved in this game at the Gtech Community Stadium following his 45-minute return in the 3-0 win against Elfsborg. Here's the Tottenham team Postecoglou has selected: Kinsky; Porro, Gray, Davies, Spence; Bentancur, Bissouma, Kulusevski, Moore, Richarlison, Son. Subs: Austin, Reguilon, Cassanova, Hardy, Olusesi, Sarr, Bergvall, Ajayi, Scarlett.

arts.ttl: Tottenham learn asking price for January target who will be sold “for the right price”
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur are looking to improve their attacking unit with the signing of Lorient striker Eli Junior Kroupi. The 18-year-old is highly rated across Europe, and he could prove to be a quality long-term investment for the North London outfit. According to Football Insider, Lorient will “consider selling him for the right price” this month. arts.ttl: League One boss makes grovelling apology after 'embarrassing' Ange Postecoglou swipe
arts.sum: Richie Wellens' comments mocking Ange Postecoglou led to anger from Tottenham fans, who pointed out the club have a close relationship with Leyton Orient and have loaned them two players this season

arts.ttl: Marcus Rashford in line for Tottenham debut after agreeing Aston Villa loan switch from Manchester United
